item.title: 这个周末你学到了什么？ | What you learning about this weekend?
item.description:
嘿伙计们👋

这个周末你们都学什么？

无论您是在提高JS技能、为您选择的 OSS 存储库制作 PR、整理您的作品集，还是在此处撰写有关 DEV 的新文章，我们都希望了解您的情况。

按照自己的节奏学习！

一旦被暂停，codenewbieteam 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，codenewbieteam 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，codenewbieteam 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己才能访问。

如果 codenewbieteam 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 codenewbieteam：

使 codenewbieteam 的所有帖子不那么明显

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 codenewbieteam 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Hey folks 👋

What y'all learning about this weekend?

Whether you're sharpening your JS skills, making PRs to your OSS repo of choice 😉, sprucing up your portfolio, or writing a new post here on DEV, we'd like to hear about it.

Learn at your own pace!

Once suspended, codenewbieteam will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, codenewbieteam will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by codenewbieteam will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If codenewbieteam is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag codenewbieteam:

Make all posts by codenewbieteam less visible

codenewbieteam consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging codenewbieteam will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: michaeltharrington

item.title: 如何在 Angular 中使用 ProvideHttpClient 获取数据 | How to Fetch Data using the provideHttpClient in Angular
item.description:
其中一项变化是引入了独立应用程序的概念，该概念是在 Angular v14 中引入的。 独立应用程序提供了创建不带模块的项目的选项。 我们可以将此类应用程序称为无模块应用程序，它允许仅导入构建应用程序所需的包。

为了在独立应用程序中获取数据，Angular 引入了使用provideHttpClient的概念。 在基于模块的应用程序中， HttpClientModule仍然是最佳选择。 在接下来的几个步骤中，我将详细介绍如何使用provideHttpClient从公共 REST API 获取数据。

先决条件

本教程要求您熟悉下列技术的基础知识：

  • 网页
  • 打字稿
  • 节点包管理器 (npm)

目录

您还可以在我的YouTube 频道上观看本文的视频版本：

如何在 Angular 16 中安装和创建独立应用程序

要创建新的 Angular 项目，我们需要确保终端中安装了 Angular。 为此，我们必须打开终端并运行命令ng version 。 如果您收到错误消息，则意味着您的计算机上尚未安装 Angular，您必须运行以下命令才能安装它：

 npm install -g @angular/cli

安装完成后，您可以关闭并重新打开终端，然后重新运行ng version命令。 这应该在终端中给出以下结果：

从上图中，我们可以看到安装的 Angular 版本的版本，截至本文撰写时为 Angular 16。

接下来，我们可以通过运行以下命令在终端中创建一个新的独立应用程序：

 ng new ng-client --routing=false --style=css --standalone

上面的命令将生成一个名为ng-client的新独立应用程序。 该应用程序将没有路由配置（因为我们将其设置为 false）和 CSS 样式表。 要运行此应用程序，我们可以导航到ng-client目录并运行ng serve --open命令。 这应该会打开默认的 Angular 模板用户界面，如下所示。

生成 Angular 服务

在此应用程序中，我们将利用 Angular 服务从 REST API 获取数据。 为此，我们需要做的第一件事是使用以下命令生成 Angular Service：

 ng g service service/data

通过上述操作，在服务文件夹内创建了一个名为DataService的新服务。

如何配置provideHttpClient

要配置 Angular 服务，我们转到main.ts文件。 在这里我们可以得到下面的代码：

 import { bootstrapApplication } from '@angular/platform-browser'; import { AppComponent } from './app/app.component'; import { provideHttpClient } from '@angular/common/http'; bootstrapApplication(AppComponent, { providers: [provideHttpClient()], }).catch((err) => console.error(err));

总结一下上面的代码，我们

  • 首先从@angular/common/http包导入provideHttpClient 。
  • 接下来，我们在providers数组中注入provideHttpClient() 。

现在，此配置使得可以利用HttpClient与DataService文件中的 REST API 进行通信。

集成 JSON 占位符 REST API

我们将在本教程中使用的公共 REST API 称为JSON Placeholder 。 要集成此 API，我们需要在DataService文件中导入几个包：

 import { HttpClient } from '@angular/common/http'; import { Observable } from 'rxjs';

接下来，我们创建一个接口，帮助塑造数据对象的呈现方式，如下所示：

 interface Post { userId: number; id: number; title: string; body: string; }

现在，我们可以创建保存 REST API 链接的变量，并在构造函数中注入HttpClient 。

 apiUrl = 'https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts'; constructor(private http: HttpClient) {}

最后，我们创建与 REST API 通信的函数，如下所示：

 getAllPosts(): Observable<Post[]> { return this.http.get<Post[]>(this.apiUrl); }
  • 在return语句中，我们使用get http 请求从 API 检索数据。

我们的DataService文件现在应如下所示：

 import { HttpClient } from '@angular/common/http'; import { Observable } from 'rxjs'; import { Injectable } from '@angular/core'; interface Post { userId: number; id: number; title: string; body: string; } @Injectable({ providedIn: 'root', }) export class DataService { apiUrl = 'https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts'; constructor(private http: HttpClient) {} getAllPosts(): Observable<Post[]> { return this.http.get<Post[]>(this.apiUrl); } }

通过此配置，我们现在可以通过使用依赖项注入直接从组件文件与服务进行通信。

在 HTML 表格中显示数据

为了在表格上显示数据，我们转到app.component.ts文件。 这里我们实现如下代码：

  • 我们首先在第 3 行导入DataService ，然后将其注入到第 24 行的构造函数中。
  • 接下来，我们在第 5 行和第 11 行之间创建了名为Post的接口。
  • 在第 21 行和第 22 行中，我们创建了两个变量。 第一个名为posts的变量被指定为Post类型，其初始值为空数组 ( [] )。 该变量将保存从 REST API 获取的数据。 第二个名为errorMessage变量将保存我们的错误消息（如果存在）。
  • 最后，在ngOnInit生命周期挂钩中，我们实现了使用DataService中的数据的逻辑。 在第 27 行中，我们从 DataService 中调用了getAllPosts()函数。 然后我们订阅了getAllPosts()函数，从订阅中返回两个Observers 。 第 28 行next调用第一个Observer 。 这里我们调用我们之前在第 21 行创建的posts变量，并将下一个参数的数据附加到它，这也称为posts 。 接下来，在第 32 行，我们创建了错误Observer ​​，然后将可能发生的任何可能的错误附加到errorMessage变量中。

通过此实现，如果我们保存所有文件，然后运行ng serve --open命令，将在浏览器中打开一个新选项卡。 在我们的app.component.ts文件中，第 30 行有一个console.log方法，它允许我们在打开控制台时查看数据，如下图所示：

要在表格内的控制台中显示数据，我们前往app.component.html ，清除样板代码，然后粘贴以下代码：

 <table> <tr> <th>id</th> <th>title</th> <th>body</th> </tr> <tr *ngFor="let post of posts"> <td>{{post.id}}</td> <td>{{post.title}}</td> <td>{{post.body}}</td> </tr> <tr> </table>

上面，我们在tr标记中使用*ngFor指令来循环数据。 然后，我们使用插值（ {{}} ）来显示id 、 title和body的数据。

如果我们保存，我们现在可以在浏览器中看到以下结果：

结论

在本教程中，您学习了如何通过使用 Angular 中的provideHttpClient来获取独立应用程序的数据。 如果您想访问代码库，您可以在Github上分叉或克隆该存储库。

如果您觉得这篇文章有帮助，您可以通过订阅我的YouTube 频道来表达您的支持，我在该频道中创建了有关 JavaScript、React、Angular、Node.js、WordPress 等 Web 开发技术的精彩教程。



原文:

The Angular echo system has been undergoing some changes over the past year. One of those changes was the introduction of the concept of Standalone applications, which was introduced in Angular v14. The Standalone applications provide the option of creating projects without modules. We can call such applications module-less applications, which allows the possibility of only importing the packages needed for building an application.

To fetch data in a Standalone application, Angular introduced the concept of using the provideHttpClient. In a module-based application the HttpClientModule remains the way to go. In the next few steps, I will break down how we can use the provideHttpClient to fetch data from a public REST API.

Prerequisites

This tutorial requires you to be familiar with the basics of the technologies listed below:

  • Html
  • JavaScript
  • TypeScript
  • node package manager (npm)

Table of Contents

You can also watch the video version of this article on my YouTube channel:

How to Install and Create a Standalone App in Angular 16

To create a new Angular project, we need to ensure we have Angular installed in the terminal. To do that, we have to open the terminal and run the command ng version. If you get an error message, that means Angular has not been installed on your machine and you'll have to run the following command below to have it installed:

npm install -g @angular/cli

Once the installation is complete, you can close and reopen the terminal, and then re-run the ng version command. That should give us the result below in the terminal:

From the image above, we can see the version of the Angular version installed, which is Angular 16 as of the time of writing of this article.

Next, we can proceed to create a new standalone application in our terminal by running the following command:

ng new ng-client --routing=false --style=css --standalone

The command above will generate a new standalone application called ng-client. The application will be without a routing configuration as we set it to false, and a style sheet of CSS. To run this application, we can navigate to the ng-client directory and run the ng serve --open command. This should open up the default Angular template user interface as seen below.

Generating the Angular service

Services in Angular are TypeScript classes that help to perform specific functions in an Angular application. In this application, we will be making use of the Angular Service to fetch data from a REST API. To do this, the first thing we need to do is to generate an Angular Service, with the following command:

ng g service service/data

With the above, a new Service called DataService is created inside a service folder.

How to Configure the provideHttpClient

To configure the Angular Service, we head to the main.ts file. Here we can have the following code below:

import { bootstrapApplication } from '@angular/platform-browser';
import { AppComponent } from './app/app.component';
import { provideHttpClient } from '@angular/common/http'; bootstrapApplication(AppComponent, { providers: [provideHttpClient()],
}).catch((err) => console.error(err));

To summarize the above code, we

  • Start by importing the provideHttpClient from the @angular/common/http package.
  • Next, we inject the provideHttpClient() within the providers array.

This configuration now makes it possible to make use of the HttpClient to communicate with the REST API in our DataService file.

Integrating the JSON Placeholder REST API

The public REST API we will use in this tutorial is called the JSON Placeholder. To integrate this API, we need to import a couple of packages in our DataService file:

import { HttpClient } from '@angular/common/http';
import { Observable } from 'rxjs';

Next, we create the interface that helps to shape the way the object from our data is going take, as seen below:

interface Post { userId: number; id: number; title: string; body: string;
}

The user id, id, title, and body are all data we intend to display in the table. We can now create the variable that holds the link of our REST API as well as inject the HttpClient in the constructor.

apiUrl = 'https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts'; constructor(private http: HttpClient) {}

Finally, we create the function that communicates with the REST API as seen below:

getAllPosts(): Observable<Post[]> { return this.http.get<Post[]>(this.apiUrl);
}
  • The getAllPosts() is appended to an Observable of type Post.
  • In the return statement, we make use of the get http request to retrieve data from the API.

Our DataService file should now look like this:

import { HttpClient } from '@angular/common/http';
import { Observable } from 'rxjs';
import { Injectable } from '@angular/core'; interface Post { userId: number; id: number; title: string; body: string;
} @Injectable({ providedIn: 'root',
})
export class DataService { apiUrl = 'https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts'; constructor(private http: HttpClient) {} getAllPosts(): Observable<Post[]> { return this.http.get<Post[]>(this.apiUrl); }
}

With this configuration, we can now communicate with the service directly from our component file by making use of dependency injection.

Displaying the Data in an HTML Table

To display the data on a table, we head to our app.component.ts file. Here we implement the following code below:

  • We begin by importing the DataService in line 3, and then injecting it in the constructor in line 24.
  • Next, we created the interface called Post between line 5 and line 11.
  • In line 21 and line 22, we created two variables. The first variable called posts, which is assigned a type of Post, has an initial value of an empty array([]). This variable will hold the data fetched from the REST API. The second variable called errorMessage will hold our error message, if any exists.
  • Finally, within our ngOnInit lifecycle hook we implement the logic to consume the data from our DataService. In line 27, we called the getAllPosts() function from our DataService. We then subscribed to the getAllPosts() function, returning two Observers from the subscription. The first Observer is called next in line 28. Here we called the posts variable we created earlier in line 21, and attached the data from the next argument to it, which is also called posts. Next, in line 32, we created the error Observer and then attached any possible error that may occur to the errorMessage variable.

With this implementation, if we save all the files and then run the ng serve --open command, a new tab will open in the browser. In our app.component.ts file, we have a console.log method in line 30, which allows us to view the data when we open the console as seen in the image below:

To display the data in the console inside of a table, we head to the app.component.html, clear the boilerplate code and then paste the code below:

<table> <tr> <th>id</th>
 <th>title</th>
 <th>body</th>
 </tr>
 <tr *ngFor="let post of posts"> <td>{{post.id}}</td>
 <td>{{post.title}}</td>
 <td>{{post.body}}</td>
 </tr>
 <tr>
</table>

Above, we made use of the *ngFor directive within the tr tag to loop through the data. We then made use of interpolation({{}}) to display data for the id, title, and the body.

If we save, we can now have the results below in the browser:

Conclusion

In this tutorial, you have learned how to fetch data for standalone applications by making use of the provideHttpClient in Angular. If you want access to the code base, you can either fork or clone the repo here on Github.

If you find this article helpful, you can kindly show your support by subscribing to my YouTube channel where I create awesome tutorials on web development technologies like JavaScript, React, Angular, Node.js, WordPress, and more.
item.author: desoga

item.title: 实现高效在线展示的十大最佳 Web 优化服务和工具 | Top 10 Best Web Optimization Services and Tools for an Efficient Online Presence
item.description:
对于希望在当今数字时代建立强大的在线形象的企业和个人来说，一个经过良好优化的网站至关重要。 网络优化是指提高网站的性能、速度和用户体验，从而带来更多流量、更好的搜索引擎排名和更高的转化率。 在本文中，我将分享一些适合您网站的最佳优化服务和工具。

它提供网站的移动和桌面版本的性能分数，突出显示需要改进的领域，例如图像优化、JavaScript 和 CSS 缩小以及缓存。

它提供有关网站速度、性能和整体优化的详细见解。 通过进行彻底的测试，GTmetrix 评估页面加载时间、页面总大小和请求数量等因素。 有了这些信息，您就可以确定需要改进的领域并实施策略来优化您的网站，以获得更快、更用户友好的体验。

凭借其智能页面缓存，它极大地提高了页面加载时间并减少了服务器响应时间。 此外，它还通过缩小 HTML、CSS 和 JavaScript 来优化代码，以提高网站的整体性能。

使用 Pingdom，您可以快速识别并解决可能妨碍用户体验的性能问题。 通过模拟来自世界各地不同地点的用户访问，Pingdom 提供了有关页面加载时间、正常运行时间和性能指标的宝贵见解。 当您的网站出现停机或响应时间缓慢时，它还会发送警报，使您能够立即采取行动并确保最佳的网站性能。

它提供了页面加载时间、瀑布图和其他关键指标的详细细分，帮助您查明性能瓶颈和需要改进的领域。 凭借其真实的浏览器测试功能，WebPageTest 可以真实地了解用户如何体验您的网站，从而使您能够针对不同的浏览器和设备进行优化。 这些宝贵的数据使您能够做出数据驱动的决策，以提高网站的速度和用户体验。

通过利用先进的压缩算法，Kraken.io 有效地减小了图像的大小，从而缩短了加载时间并提高了网站的整体速度。 凭借其用户友好的界面和 API 集成，Kraken.io 提供了批量或即时优化图像的无缝体验。 通过降低网站图像的带宽和存储要求，Kraken.io 有助于提供更好的用户体验并提高搜索引擎排名。

凭借其先进的技术，Yottaa 通过减少页面加载时间、提高服务器响应时间和最大限度地减少页面膨胀来优化您的网站。 通过利用全球内容交付点网络，Yottaa 可确保您的网站内容快速高效地交付给用户，无论用户身在何处。 凭借前端优化和动态内容加速等附加功能，Yottaa 提供了一种整体的 Web 优化方法，从而带来更快、更具吸引力的用户体验。

通过缓存静态内容并将其分布到全球服务器网络，Cloudflare 提高了网站性能并降低了服务器负载。 它还提供其他优化功能，例如图像优化、CSS 和 JavaScript 文件缩小以及 HTTP/2 优先级。 此外，Cloudflare 通过防御 DDoS 攻击、提供 SSL 加密和实施 Web 应用程序防火墙 (WAF) 规则来增强网站安全性。

凭借其强大的关键字研究功能，SEMrush 可帮助您识别高价值关键字并优化您的内容以获得更好的可见性。 它还提供竞争对手分析、反向链接审核和页面优化工具，帮助您在竞争激烈的数字领域保持领先地位。 此外，SEMrush 还提供有关有机流量、站点审核和位置跟踪的宝贵见解，使您能够监控和衡量优化工作的有效性。

借助热图、访客记录和转化渠道等功能，Hotjar 可以提供有关用户参与度、导航模式和需要改进的领域的宝贵见解。 通过分析这些数据，您可以做出数据驱动的决策来优化网站的设计、布局和用户体验，最终提高转化率和整体网站性能。

优化您的网站对于成功的在线展示至关重要，利用正确的网络优化服务和工具可以显着影响您网站的性能、用户体验和搜索引擎排名。🎈

我希望您觉得这篇文章令人愉快且富有洞察力！ 如果您喜欢，请随时发表评论并分享您的想法。 谢谢你！🥰



原文:

A well-optimized website is essential for businesses and individuals looking to build a strong online presence in today's digital age. Web optimization is about improving the performance, speed and user experience of your website, resulting in more traffic, better search engine rankings and better conversion rates. In this article I will share some of the best optimization services and tools for your website.

1. Google PageSpeed Insights

Google PageSpeed Insights is a free tool that analyzes the content of a web page and provides suggestions to make it faster. It offers performance scores for both mobile and desktop versions of your website, highlighting areas for improvement, such as image optimization, JavaScript and CSS minification, and caching.

2. GTmetrix

GTmetrix is a powerful web optimization tool designed to help you analyze and enhance your website's performance. It provides detailed insights into your website's speed, performance, and overall optimization. By conducting thorough tests, GTmetrix evaluates factors such as page load time, total page size, and the number of requests. Armed with this information, you can identify areas for improvement and implement strategies to optimize your website for a faster, more user-friendly experience.

3. Nitropack.io

NitroPack.io is a powerful web optimization service designed to supercharge your website's performance. With its intelligent page caching, it dramatically improves page load times and reduces server response time. NitroPack.io also includes image optimization capabilities, automatically compressing and resizing images for faster loading. Additionally, it optimizes code by minifying HTML, CSS, and JavaScript to enhance overall website performance.

4. Pingdom

Pingdom is a renowned web optimization tool that offers real-time monitoring and performance testing capabilities for your website. With Pingdom, you can quickly identify and resolve performance issues that may hinder user experience. By simulating user visits from different locations around the world, Pingdom provides valuable insights into page load times, uptime, and performance metrics. It also sends alerts when your website experiences downtime or slow response times, allowing you to take immediate action and ensure optimal website performance.

5. WebPageTest

WebPageTest is a powerful and comprehensive web optimization tool that allows you to analyze your website's performance from various locations and devices. It provides a detailed breakdown of page load times, waterfall charts, and other critical metrics, helping you pinpoint performance bottlenecks and areas for improvement. With its real browser testing capabilities, WebPageTest offers a realistic view of how users experience your website, allowing you to optimize for different browsers and devices. This valuable data empowers you to make data-driven decisions to enhance your website's speed and user experience.

6. Kraken.io

Kraken.io is a powerful image optimization tool that helps you enhance your website's performance by reducing image file sizes without compromising quality. By utilizing advanced compression algorithms, Kraken.io effectively reduces the size of your images, resulting in faster loading times and improved overall website speed. With its user-friendly interface and API integration, Kraken.io offers a seamless experience for optimizing images in bulk or on-the-fly. By reducing the bandwidth and storage requirements for your website's images, Kraken.io contributes to a better user experience and improved search engine rankings.

7. Yottaa

Yottaa is a comprehensive web optimization service that focuses on improving website performance and user experience. With its advanced technology, Yottaa optimizes your website by reducing page load times, enhancing server response times, and minimizing page bloat. By leveraging a global network of content delivery points, Yottaa ensures that your website's content is delivered to users quickly and efficiently, regardless of their location. With additional features such as front-end optimization and dynamic content acceleration, Yottaa provides a holistic approach to web optimization, resulting in a faster and more engaging user experience.

8. Cloudflare

Cloudflare is a leading content delivery network (CDN) that offers a range of web optimization and security features. By caching static content and distributing it across a global network of servers, Cloudflare improves website performance and reduces server load. It also provides additional optimization features such as image optimization, minification of CSS and JavaScript files, and HTTP/2 prioritization. Moreover, Cloudflare enhances website security by protecting against DDoS attacks, offering SSL encryption, and implementing web application firewall (WAF) rules.

9. SEMrush

SEMrush is a comprehensive SEO toolset that offers a wide range of features to optimize your website and improve its search engine rankings. With its robust keyword research capabilities, SEMrush helps you identify high-value keywords and optimize your content for better visibility. It also offers competitor analysis, backlink auditing, and on-page optimization tools to help you stay ahead in the competitive digital landscape. Additionally, SEMrush provides valuable insights into organic traffic, site audits, and position tracking, allowing you to monitor and measure the effectiveness of your optimization efforts.

10. Hotjar

Hotjar is a powerful user behavior analytics tool that allows you to visualize and understand how users interact with your website. With features like heat maps, visitor recordings, and conversion funnels, Hotjar provides valuable insights into user engagement, navigation patterns, and areas for improvement. By analyzing this data, you can make data-driven decisions to optimize your website's design, layout, and user experience, ultimately enhancing conversion rates and overall website performance.

Optimizing your website is essential for a successful online presence, and utilizing the right web optimization services and tools can significantly impact your website's performance, user experience, and search engine rankings.🎈

I hope you found this article enjoyable and insightful! If you liked it, please feel free to leave a comment and share your thoughts. Thank you!🥰
item.author: vishnusatheesh

item.title: 如何使用 React、TypeScript 和样式组件制作文本组件 | How To Make Text Component with React, TypeScript and Styled Components
item.description:
文本可能是任何应用程序中最常用的组件。 今天我想分享一个可靠的可重用文本组件，它在我的前端开发工作中非常有用，也应该为您节省时间。

文本组件

此外，我们可以以任何我们喜欢的方式修改它，使用styles属性或使用styled函数包装它。 由于这种灵活性，它可以作为其他组件的构建块。

 import styled, { DefaultTheme, css } from "styled-components" const getTextColorRecord = ({ colors }: DefaultTheme) => ({ regular: colors.text, supporting: colors.textSupporting, supporting2: colors.textSupporting2, supporting3: colors.textSupporting3, primary: colors.primary, attention: colors.attention, alert: colors.alert, success: colors.success, reversed: colors.background, contrast: colors.contrast, } as const) type TextWeight = "regular" | "semibold" | "bold" const fontWeight: Record<TextWeight, number> = { regular: 400, semibold: 500, bold: 600, } type TextHeight = "small" | "regular" | "large" const lineHeight: Record<TextHeight, number> = { small: 1, regular: 1.2, large: 1.5, } export type TextColor = keyof ReturnType<typeof getTextColorRecord> export interface TextProps { color?: TextColor weight?: TextWeight size?: number height?: TextHeight centered?: boolean cropped?: boolean nowrap?: boolean } export const oneRemInPx = 16 const getFontSize = (sizeInPx: number) => { const sizeInRem = sizeInPx / oneRemInPx return `${sizeInRem}rem` } export const Text = styled.p<TextProps>` margin: 0; padding: 0; overflow-wrap: break-word; ${({ color, theme }) => color && css` color: ${getTextColorRecord(theme)[color].toCssValue()}; `} ${({ weight }) => weight && css` font-weight: ${fontWeight[weight]}; `} ${({ height }) => height && css` line-height: ${lineHeight[height]}; `} ${({ size }) => size && css` font-size: ${getFontSize(size)}; `} ${({ centered }) => centered && css` text-align: center; `} ${({ nowrap }) => nowrap && css` white-space: nowrap; `} ${({ cropped }) => cropped && css` white-space: nowrap; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; `} `

为了保持 UI 一致性，我们在Text样式开始时重置了默认的浏览器 CSS，方法是删除边距和填充，并将overflow-wrap设置为break-word ，以防止文本溢出破坏布局。 然后我们继续处理它的属性。

让我们看看可以传递给Text组件的额外属性。 虽然我们可以通过样式或类名来设置颜色，但大多数情况下我们会使用color属性。 它是一组有限的命名颜色，例如常规颜色、支持颜色或警报颜色。 我们通过getTextColorRecord函数访问所有这些函数，而该函数又依赖于 styled-components 主题。 请注意，所有名称都描述颜色，而不是说黄色或白色。 这样我们就可以更改调色板并添加新主题，而无需更改Text组件。

但您也可以根据自己的情况进行扩展，例如thin或extrabold选项。

当我们将像素作为数字传递时，它会被转换为雷姆。 对我们来说，用像素来思考更容易。 我们认为16px作为默认大小，为了制作更小的变体，传递14px而不是0.875rem更直观。 同时，我们通过将像素转换为 rems 来使应用程序更易于访问，以便用户可以在浏览器设置中更改字体大小，应用程序也会相应地进行调整。

虽然有些人更喜欢使用文本变体，但根据我的经验，我发现依靠size和weight等属性来创建各种文本样式更为实用。 然而，如果您的项目将受益于变体，您可以将其添加为属性，然后记录每个变体的样式。

最后还有centered 、 cropped和nowrap帮助器，它们是不言自明的。

一旦被暂停，rodionchachura 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，rodionchachura 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，rodionchachura 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己才能访问。

如果 rodionchachura 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 rodionchachura：

使 rodionchachura 的所有帖子不那么明显

举报其他不当行为

坚持不懈的 rodionchachura 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

✨ Watch on YouTube | 🐙 GitHub | 🎮 Demo

Text is likely the most used component in any app. Today I want to share a solid reusable Text component that is very useful in my front-end development work and should save time for you too.

Text Component

The Text component is just a styled paragraph that we can also turn into any other element like div, span, or heading by passing the as property. Also, we can modify it any way we like, using the styles property or wrapping it with the styled function. Because of that flexibility, it serves as a building block for other components.

import styled, { DefaultTheme, css } from "styled-components" const getTextColorRecord = ({ colors }: DefaultTheme) => ({ regular: colors.text, supporting: colors.textSupporting, supporting2: colors.textSupporting2, supporting3: colors.textSupporting3, primary: colors.primary, attention: colors.attention, alert: colors.alert, success: colors.success, reversed: colors.background, contrast: colors.contrast, } as const) type TextWeight = "regular" | "semibold" | "bold"
const fontWeight: Record<TextWeight, number> = { regular: 400, semibold: 500, bold: 600,
} type TextHeight = "small" | "regular" | "large"
const lineHeight: Record<TextHeight, number> = { small: 1, regular: 1.2, large: 1.5,
} export type TextColor = keyof ReturnType<typeof getTextColorRecord> export interface TextProps { color?: TextColor weight?: TextWeight size?: number height?: TextHeight centered?: boolean cropped?: boolean nowrap?: boolean
} export const oneRemInPx = 16 const getFontSize = (sizeInPx: number) => { const sizeInRem = sizeInPx / oneRemInPx return `${sizeInRem}rem`
} export const Text = styled.p<TextProps>` margin: 0; padding: 0; overflow-wrap: break-word; ${({ color, theme }) => color && css` color: ${getTextColorRecord(theme)[color].toCssValue()}; `} ${({ weight }) => weight && css` font-weight: ${fontWeight[weight]}; `} ${({ height }) => height && css` line-height: ${lineHeight[height]}; `} ${({ size }) => size && css` font-size: ${getFontSize(size)}; `} ${({ centered }) => centered && css` text-align: center; `} ${({ nowrap }) => nowrap && css` white-space: nowrap; `} ${({ cropped }) => cropped && css` white-space: nowrap; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; `}
`

For UI consistency we reset default browser CSS at the beginning of the Text styling by removing margings and padding and setting overflow-wrap to break-word to prevent text overflow from breaking the layout. Then we continue by handling it's properties.

Let's look into extra properties that we can pass to the Text component. While we can set set color through styles or className, most often we will use the color property. It's a limited set of named colors, such as regular, supporting, or alert. We access all of them through the getTextColorRecord function that in turn relies on the styled-components theme. Note, that all the names describe the color rather that saying ellow or white. That way we can change the color palette and add new themes without changing the Text component.

The weight property turns regular, semibold and bold into 400, 500, 600. It's usually enough for most cases. But you can also extends it for you situation, andd thin or extrabold options for example.

The size is an interesting and opinionated property. While we pass a pixels as a number it's being converted to rems. For us it's easier to think in pixels. We think of 16px as a default size, and to make a smaller variant it's more intuitive to pass 14px rather than 0.875rem. At the same time we make the app more accessible by converting pixels to rems, so that users can change the font size in their browser settings and the app will adjust accordingly.

The height property represents line-height and can be small, regular, or large, yet we are rarely going to use it.

While some people prefer having variants for texts, from my experience I found it more practical to rely on properties like size and weight to create a variety of text styles. Yet, if your project would benefit from having variants, you can add it as a property, and then have a record of styles for each variant.

Finally there are centered, cropped, and nowrap helpers that are self-explanatory.

Once suspended, rodionchachura will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, rodionchachura will be able to comment and publish posts again.

If rodionchachura is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Make all posts by rodionchachura less visible

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging rodionchachura will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: 更漂亮以及如何充分利用它 | Prettier and how to get most out of it
item.description:
介绍

什么是更漂亮？ 必须强调的是，Prettier 是固执己见的，因为它提供的可配置属性非常少（截至 2023 年 6 月 26 日为 23 个）。

代码格式化是什么意思？ 可以通过以下任一方式完成：

安装 Prettier 插件（强烈推荐）和/或

运行 prettier --write 。 命令

更漂亮的规则和最佳实践

在配置 Prettier 设置时，建议优先考虑简单性，因为该工具的理念植根于其固执己见的本质。 与其他工具更好的集成

这非常方便，因为您不必再​​费心手动格式化代码。 更漂亮和管道/哈士奇

由于并非所有同事都必须安装该插件并确保代码已格式化，因此需要自动强制执行。 为此，建议使用 husky 运行 Prettier 预提交。 有关如何执行此操作的教程，请参见此处 - prettier.io/docs/en/precommit.html 。 此外，添加一个更漂亮的检查可能是值得的。 在您的管道中只是为了确保代码符合 Prettier 规则。

通过结合 Prettier 和 ESLint，您可以利用这两种工具的优势。 另一方面，ESLint 提供了强大的配置功能来加强代码质量和最佳实践。

它使用 Prettier 格式化您的代码，然后应用 eslint --fix 命令以确保遵守您的 eslint 配置。 这使您可以受益于 Prettier 强大的格式化功能，同时保持与 eslint 的 linting 一致性。

更漂亮和 XML

默认情况下 Prettier 不支持 XML，如果您确实需要 Prettier 支持 XML，有一个插件可以帮助您。 不幸的是，Pretteir XML 不能与 Prettier 设置的其余部分一起开箱即用，因此您需要进行一些额外的配置。

 module.exports = { overrides: [ { files: "*/", options: { "bracketSpacing": false, "singleQuote": true, "trailingComma": "es5", "arrowParens": "avoid" } }, { files: "*.xml", options: { parser: "xml", plugins: ["@prettier/plugin-xml"], tabWidth: 4, printWidth: 120, bracketSameLine: true, singleAttributePerLine: true, useTabs: true } } ] };

当你在 husky 中调用它时，你可以执行类似的操作来确保 xml 已格式化 -

更漂亮的替代品

尽管 Prettier 已成为流行的代码格式化程序，ESLint 代码格式化仍然是一个有价值的选择。 首先，ESLint 涵盖更广泛的范围，允许进行代码格式化以及用于检测潜在错误和实施最佳实践的静态分析。 这种灵活性有利于保持一致的代码质量并防止常见错误。 其次，ESLint 提供了一个庞大的插件和配置生态系统，使开发人员能够根据其特定的项目需求自定义和定制 linting 规则。 这种级别的可配置性确保了对不同代码库和编码风格的适应性。 因此，ESLint 仍然是代码格式化等方面可靠而强大的工具。- www.npmjs.com/package/eslint ，

标准

它通过将代码格式化与一组预定义的 linting 规则相结合来提供全面的解决方案。 这些规则不仅包含代码风格约定，还包含潜在的错误和最佳实践。 标准的预定义特性消除了大量配置的需要，使其设置快速、简单。 因此，对于寻求代码格式化和 linting 一体化解决方案的开发人员来说，Standard 仍然是一个可靠的选择。

与 Prettier 相比，JS-Beautify 仍然是一个可行的选择。 它专注于代码格式化，并为缩进、间距和其他风格首选项提供广泛的自定义选项。 它支持各种编程语言，并提供处理不同代码库的灵活性。 因此，JS-Beautify 与 Prettier 一样，仍然是代码格式化需求的有力竞争者。

结论

总之，充分利用 Prettier 的潜力需要接受其固执己见的本质并保持配置尽可能简单。 通过遵循 Prettier 的理念，开发人员可以从一致且美观的代码格式中受益。 将 Prettier 与 ESLint 等其他工具集成可以提高代码质量并遵守最佳实践。 虽然 ESLint、Standard 和 JS-Beautify 等替代选项提供了不同的功能和定制级别，但 Prettier 仍然是实现代码可读性和美观性的可靠选择。 最终，选择正确的代码格式化工具取决于开发团队的具体需求和偏好。



原文:

Intro

What is Prettier? Prettier is an opinionated code formatter that supports many languages and integrates with most of code editors. It must be emphasised that Prettier is opinionated since it provides very few configurable properties (23 as of 26th June 2023).

What does code formatting mean? Essential Prettier parses and reprints code according to the rules set in the config file. It can be done by either:

Installing Prettier plugin (highly recommended) and/or

Running prettier --write . command

Prettier rules and best practices

When it comes to configuring Prettier settings, it is advisable to prioritize simplicity, as the tool's philosophy is rooted in its opinionated nature. Google's TypeScript Style guide also reflects this approach, recommending keeping Prettier configurations minimal to fully embrace its opinionated formatting decisions.

// https://github.com/google/gts/blob/main/.prettierrc.json
{
  "bracketSpacing": false,
  "singleQuote": true,
  "trailingComma": "es5",
  "arrowParens": "avoid"
}

Prettier integrations with other tools

Prettier plugin (VSCode and most other code editors)

Prettier plugin will generally allow you to set up your code editor to reformat the code in your file every time you save it. This is very handy since you don't have to bother with formatting your code by hand any more. prettier.io/docs/en/editors.html

Prettier and pipeline/ husky

Since not all of your colleagues will neccecerly have set up the plugin and will make sure that the code is formatted it is needed to enforce that automatically. To do that it is recommended to use husky to run Prettier pre-commit. For a tutorial on how to do it, look here- prettier.io/docs/en/precommit.html . Additionally, it might be worthwhile to add a prettier check . in your pipeline just to be sure that the code meets the Prettier rules.

Prettier and ESLint

By combining Prettier and ESLint, you can leverage the strengths of both tools. Prettier is responsible for formatting your code, ensuring consistent and aesthetically pleasing styles. On the other hand, ESLint provides powerful configuration capabilities to enforce code quality and best practices.

prettier-eslint plugin combines the formatting capabilities of Prettier with the configuration capabilities of eslint. It formats your code using Prettier and then applies eslint --fix command to ensure adherence to your eslint configuration. This allows you to benefit from Prettier's powerful formatting while maintaining linting consistency with eslint.

github.com/prettier/prettier-eslint

eslint-plugin-prettier plugin is an ESLint plugin that runs Prettier as an ESLint rule and reports differences as individual ESLint issues.

www.npmjs.com/package/eslint-plugin-pre…

Prettier and XML

By default Prettier does not support XML, if you do need Prettier to support XML there is a plugin in place that can help you out. www.npmjs.com/package/@prettier/plugin-…

Unfortunately, Pretteir XML does not work out of the box together with the rest of your Prettier setup, therefore you need to do some additional configuration.

         module.exports = {
            overrides: [
                {
                    files: "*/",
                    options: {
                       "bracketSpacing": false,
                       "singleQuote": true,
                       "trailingComma": "es5",
                       "arrowParens": "avoid"
                      }        
                },
                {
                    files: "*.xml",
                    options: {
                        parser: "xml",
                        plugins: ["@prettier/plugin-xml"], 
                        tabWidth: 4,
                        printWidth: 120, 
                        bracketSameLine: true,
                        singleAttributePerLine: true, 
                        useTabs: true
                    }
                }
            ]
          };

And when you call it in husky you can do something like this to make sure that xml is formatted-

"prettier:xml": "prettier --parser xml --config ./prettier.config.js --write '*/.xml'"

Prettier alternatives

ESLint

ESLint code formatting is still a valuable choice despite the emergence of Prettier as a popular code formatter. Firstly, ESLint encompasses a broader scope, allowing for code formatting as well as static analysis for detecting potential errors and enforcing best practices. This flexibility is advantageous for maintaining consistent code quality and preventing common mistakes. Secondly, ESLint provides a vast ecosystem of plugins and configurations, empowering developers to customize and tailor linting rules to their specific project needs. This level of configurability ensures adaptability to diverse codebases and coding styles. Therefore, ESLint remains a reliable and powerful tool for code formatting and beyond.- www.npmjs.com/package/eslint,

Standard

Standard is still a strong alternative to Prettier. It provides a comprehensive solution by combining code formatting with a set of predefined linting rules. These rules encompass not only code style conventions but also potential errors and best practices. Standard's predefined nature eliminates the need for extensive configuration, making it quick and easy to set up. While Prettier focuses solely on code formatting, Standard offers a holistic approach that ensures code quality, consistency, and adherence to industry standards. Therefore, Standard remains a reliable choice for developers seeking an all-in-one solution for code formatting and linting.

www.npmjs.com/package/standard ,

js-beautify

JS-Beautify is still a viable option compared to Prettier. It specializes in code formatting and offers extensive customization options for indentation, spacing, and other stylistic preferences. While Prettier focuses on enforcing a consistent formatting style, JS-Beautify allows developers to tailor the formatting rules to their specific needs. It supports various programming languages and provides flexibility in handling different codebases. JS-Beautify's ability to fine-tune formatting rules makes it a valuable choice for developers who prioritize customization while maintaining code readability and aesthetics. Therefore, JS-Beautify remains a strong contender for code formatting needs alongside Prettier.

www.npmjs.com/package/js-beautify

Conclusion

In conclusion, leveraging the full potential of Prettier involves embracing its opinionated nature and keeping the configuration as simple as possible. By following Prettier's philosophy, developers can benefit from consistent and aesthetically pleasing code formatting. Integrating Prettier with other tools like ESLint enhances code quality and adherence to best practices. While alternative options such as ESLint, Standard, and JS-Beautify offer different features and levels of customization, Prettier remains a reliable choice for achieving code readability and aesthetics. Ultimately, selecting the right code formatting tool depends on the specific needs and preferences of the development team.
item.author: withtoms

item.title: 让我们通过制作 RPG 来学习《戈多 4》——第 13 部分：敌人死亡时掉落战利品🤠 | Let’s Learn Godot 4 by Making an RPG — Part 13: Enemy Dropping Loot on Death🤠
item.description:
我们终于可以射杀敌人了，但我们可以做得更好。 我希望我们升级敌人的死亡功能，以便敌人掉落我们可以拾取的战利品。 该战利品掉落将以 90% 的几率生成，并且战利品将从我们的 Pickups 枚举中随机生成。 这意味着我们的敌人可能会掉落弹药、健康饮料或耐力饮料。

您将在本部分中学到什么：

  • 如何随机化百分比和枚举返回值。

这部分会很快，所以让我们打开我们的敌人场景并开始。 首先，我们需要在现有变量下实例化我们的 Pickups 场景。

 ### Enemy.gd # older code # Pickup scene var pickups_scene = preload("res://Scenes/Pickup.tscn")

现在，在 Damage() 函数的死亡条件中，我们需要使用随机方法来随机化战利品变化百分比。 如果该值小于 0.9 (90%)，我们将随机拾取。 这个拾取器也将根据我们拥有的枚举值的数量（即 3）进行随机化。然后，这个拾取器将被添加到我们的场景中并在我们杀死的敌人的位置生成。

 ### Enemy.gd # older code #will damage the enemy when they get hit func hit(damage): health -= damage if health > 0: #damage $AnimationPlayer.play("damage") else: #death #we play the death animation and $AnimatedSprite2D.play("death") #stop movement $Timer.stop() direction = Vector2.ZERO #stop health regeneration set_process(false) #trigger animation finished signal is_attacking = true #we emit the signal for the spawner. death.emit() #drop loot randomly at a 90% chance if rng.randf() < 0.9: var pickup = pickups_scene.instantiate() pickup.item = rng.randi() % 3 #we have three pickups in our enum get_tree().root.get_node("Main").call_deferred("add_child", pickup) pickup.position = position

您的代码应该如下所示。

如果你现在运行你的场景并杀死你的敌人，他们应该有 90% 的几率掉落战利品。 如果你愿意，你可以将此值降低到 50% 或 60% - 根据你希望敌人在死亡时掉落战利品的可能性的偏好来设置它。

既然我们的敌人会掉落战利品，我们就不必不断地放下拾取物。 在下一部分中，我们将为敌人添加功能，使其能够向玩家射击并伤害我们。 请记住保存您的项目，我们下一部分见。

教程发布

其他部分的链接

该教程系列共有 23 章。 我将在接下来的几周内以每日部分的形式发布所有章节。 您可以在此处找到本系列所有 23 个部分的教程链接的更新列表。

完整教程

如果您喜欢本系列或想跳过等待并访问该教程系列的离线完整版，您可以通过在Ko-fi 上购买离线小册子仅需 3 美元来支持我！ 😊 😊



原文:

We can finally shoot and kill our enemy, but we can do one better. I want us to upgrade our enemy’s death function so that our enemy drops loot that we can pick up. This loot drop will spawn at a 90% chance, and the loot will be randomized from our Pickups enum. This means our enemy might drop either ammo, a health drink, or a stamina drink.

WHAT YOU WILL LEARN IN THIS PART:

  • How to randomize percentage and Enum return values.

This part will be quick, so let’s open our Enemy scene and get started. First, we need to instance our Pickups scene underneath our existing variables.

 ### Enemy.gd
 # older code
 # Pickup scene
 var pickups_scene = preload("res://Scenes/Pickup.tscn")

Now, in our death conditional in our damage() function, we need to use our randomized method to randomize the loot change percentage. If the value is less than 0.9 (90%), we will randomize a pickup. This pickup will also be randomized according to the number of enum values we have, which is 3. This pickup will then be added to our scene and spawned at our killed enemy’s location.

 ### Enemy.gd
 # older code
 #will damage the enemy when they get hit
 func hit(damage): health -= damage if health > 0: #damage
 $AnimationPlayer.play("damage") else: #death
 #we play the death animation and
 $AnimatedSprite2D.play("death") #stop movement
 $Timer.stop() direction = Vector2.ZERO #stop health regeneration
 set_process(false) #trigger animation finished signal
 is_attacking = true #we emit the signal for the spawner.  death.emit() #drop loot randomly at a 90% chance
 if rng.randf() < 0.9: var pickup = pickups_scene.instantiate() pickup.item = rng.randi() % 3 #we have three pickups in our enum
 get_tree().root.get_node("Main").call_deferred("add_child", pickup) pickup.position = position

Your code should look like this.

If you now run your scene and you kill your enemy, they should drop loot at a 90% chance. You can lower this value to 50% or 60% if you want — set it according to your preference of how probable you want your enemy to drop loot on death.

Now that our enemy drops loot, we don’t have to continuously place down pickups. In the next part we will add the functionality to our enemy to be able to shoot at our player and damage us. Remember to save your project, and I’ll see you in the next part.

TUTORIAL RELEASE

LINKS TO OTHER PARTS

The tutorial series has 23 chapters. I’ll be releasing all of the chapters in sectional daily parts over the next couple of weeks. You can find the updated list of the tutorial links for all 23 parts in this series here.

FULL TUTORIAL

If you like this series or want to skip the wait and access the offline, full version of the tutorial series, you can support me by buying the offline booklet for just $3 on Ko-fi!😊
item.author: christinec_dev

item.title: RFID 访问控制：它是什么以及它如何工作？ | RFID Access Control: What Is It & How Does It Work?
item.description:
发布于 7 月 1 日

这种先进的系统使用 RFID 标签进行非接触式身份验证，取代了传统的基于密钥的方法。 当用户将RFID 标签展示给读取器时，读取器会发出无线电波，为标签供电并使其能够传输其唯一标识符。 阅读器捕获该标识符并将其发送到访问控制面板进行验证。 根据验证结果，授予或拒绝访问权限。 什么是RFID门禁？

通过用 RFID 标签或卡取代传统钥匙，该系统为个人提供了一种非接触式便捷的进入方式。 当授权用户接近接入点时，他们只需向读卡器出示其 RFID 标签或卡即可。 阅读器发射无线电波为标签供电，使其能够将唯一的标识符传输回阅读器。 然后，访问控制系统会验证该标识符，并根据预定义的权限授予或拒绝访问权限。

标签通常附着在门禁卡或钥匙扣上，并在靠近 RFID 读取器时发出唯一的标识符。

  • 它们安装在入口点并负责验证访问请求。

  • 访问控制面板：该集中控制单元管理 RFID 读取器和访问控制系统之间的通信。 它处理访问请求并根据预定义的规则和用户权限授予或拒绝访问。

  • 管理软件：该软件允许管理员配置和管理访问控制参数、定义访问策略以及跟踪访问事件和日志。

    • 注册：向用户提供与其身份唯一关联的 RFID 标签或门禁卡。 这些标签用其访问权限和个人信息进行编码。

  • 身份验证过程：当用户接近接入点时，他们向 RFID 读取器出示其 RFID 标签。 阅读器发射无线电波为标签供电，使其能够将其唯一标识符传输回阅读器。

  • 数据验证： RFID阅读器捕获标签的标识符并将其发送到访问控制面板进行验证。 该面板将标识符与授权用户数据库进行比较，以确定是否应授予访问权限。

  • 允许或拒绝访问：根据验证结果，门禁控制器发送信号以解锁门或拒绝访问。 如果用户的标签有效并且具有适当的访问权限，则将授予用户进入权限。

  • 审核和监控：访问控制系统记录访问事件，维护谁在何时访问哪个区域的记录。 可以出于安全目的、审计或调查事件的目的审查此信息。

    • 增强的安全性：RFID 访问控制系统提供更高级别的安全性，降低未经授权的访问和潜在安全漏洞的风险。

  • 便捷的非接触式访问：RFID 标签可实现快速、免提访问，无需物理钥匙或手动输入。

  • 灵活性和可扩展性：RFID 系统灵活且易于扩展，可以根据组织需求的变化轻松添加或删除用户和访问权限。

  • 高效的访问管理：RFID 系统简化了访问管理流程，可以轻松分配、修改或撤销访问权限。

  • 审计跟踪和报告：RFID 访问控制系统维护访问事件的详细日志，为合规性、事件调查和分析提供全面的审计跟踪。

  • 集成能力：RFID系统可以与其他业务系统集成，优化数据共享和自动化流程，从而提高效率。

  • 增强问责制：RFID 标签将访问事件与特定个人关联起来，促进问责制并促进责任方的识别。

  • 节省成本：RFID 系统可以消除与物理钥匙、锁更换和管理任务相关的费用，从而实现长期成本节省。

    • 一旦暂停，poxorfidautomation 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被取消。

    一旦解除暂停，poxorfidautomation 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦取消发布，poxorfidautomation 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己才能访问。

    如果 poxorfidautomation 没有暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

    笔记：

    感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 poxorfidautomation：

    使 poxorfidautomation 的所有帖子不那么明显

    举报其他不当行为

    取消标记 poxorfidautomation 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



    原文:

    POXO RFID AUTOMATION

    Posted on Jul 1

     

    RFID access control is a cutting-edge technology that revolutionizes the way organizations manage access to their premises. This advanced system replaces traditional key-based methods with contactless authentication using RFID tags. RFID access control consists of key components, including RFID tags, readers, access control panels, and management software. When a user presents their RFID tag to a reader, it emits radio waves that power the tag and enables it to transmit its unique identifier. The reader captures this identifier and sends it to the access control panel for verification. Based on the validation results, access is either granted or denied. RFID access control offers enhanced security, convenience, and flexibility, making it an ideal solution for efficient access management in various settings.

    What is RFID Access Control?

    RFID access control is a sophisticated security system that utilizes Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) technology to regulate and monitor access to physical spaces. By replacing traditional keys with RFID tags or cards, this system offers a contactless and convenient way for individuals to gain entry. When authorized users approach an access point, they simply present their RFID tag or card to the reader. The reader emits radio waves that power the tag, allowing it to transmit a unique identifier back to the reader. This identifier is then validated by the access control system, which grants or denies access based on predefined permissions.

    The Components of RFID Access Control:

    1. RFID Tags: These small electronic devices consist of an integrated circuit and an antenna. Tags are typically attached to access cards or key fobs and emit a unique identifier when in proximity to an RFID reader.

    2. RFID Readers: These devices capture and interpret the data transmitted by RFID tags. They are installed at entry points and are responsible for validating access requests.

    3. Access Control Panel: This centralized control unit manages the communication between the RFID readers and the access control system. It processes access requests and grants or denies access based on predefined rules and user permissions.

    4. Management Software: This software allows administrators to configure and manage access control parameters, define access policies, and track access events and logs.

    How does an RFID access control system work?

    1. Enrollment: Users are provided with RFID tags or access cards that are uniquely associated with their identities. These tags are encoded with their access privileges and personal information.

    2. Authentication Process: When a user approaches an access point, they present their RFID tag to the RFID reader. The reader emits radio waves that power the tag, enabling it to transmit its unique identifier back to the reader.

    3. Data Verification: The RFID reader captures the tag's identifier and sends it to the access control panel for verification. The panel compares the identifier against the authorized user database to determine if access should be granted.

    4. Access Granting or Denial: Based on the verification results, the access control panel sends a signal to either unlock the door or deny access. The user is granted entry if their tag is valid and they have appropriate access permissions.

    5. Audit and Monitoring: The access control system logs access events, maintaining a record of who accessed which area and at what time. This information can be reviewed for security purposes, auditing, or investigating incidents.

    Benefits of RFID access control systems.

    1. Enhanced Security: RFID access control systems provide a higher level of security, reducing the risk of unauthorized access and potential security breaches.

    2. Convenient and Contactless Access: RFID tags allow for quick and hands-free access, eliminating the need for physical keys or manual entry.

    3. Flexibility and Scalability: RFID systems are flexible and easily scalable, allowing for easy addition or removal of users and access permissions as organizational needs evolve.

    4. Efficient Access Management: RFID systems streamline access management processes, enabling easy assignment, modification, or revocation of access privileges.

    5. Audit Trails and Reporting: RFID access control systems maintain detailed logs of access events, providing a comprehensive audit trail for compliance, incident investigation, and analysis.

    6. Integration Capabilities: RFID systems can integrate with other business systems, optimizing data sharing and automating processes for improved efficiency.

    7. Increased Accountability: RFID tags associate access events with specific individuals, promoting accountability and facilitating the identification of responsible parties.

    8. Cost Savings: RFID systems can lead to long-term cost savings by eliminating expenses related to physical keys, lock replacements, and administrative tasks.

    POXO RFID access control provides enhanced security, streamlined access management, and detailed audit trails, making it a valuable solution for organizations seeking efficient and secure access control measures.

    Once suspended, poxorfidautomation will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

    Once unsuspended, poxorfidautomation will be able to comment and publish posts again.

    Once unpublished, all posts by poxorfidautomation will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

    If poxorfidautomation is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

    Note:

    Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag poxorfidautomation:

    Make all posts by poxorfidautomation less visible

    poxorfidautomation consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

    Report other inappropriate conduct

    Unflagging poxorfidautomation will restore default visibility to their posts.

原文:

In this rapidly evolving world of Blockchain and cryptocurrency, businesses are seeking efficient and cost-effective ways to enter the market. In that case, launching a cryptocurrency exchange platform has become a lucrative business opportunity. However, building an exchange from scratch can be a time-consuming and costly process. This is where Crypto Exchange Clone Scripts come into play.

The Cryptocurrency exchange clone scripts provide the ready-made solution that replicates the features and functionalities of an established trading platform. These pre-built software solutions offer a wide range of benefits that can help entrepreneurs quickly and efficiently launch their own exchange platforms.

Here We will explore the benefits of choosing a crypto exchange script and highlight the top 5 crypto exchange clone scripts available in the market.

Benefits of Choosing Cryptocurrency Exchange Script

Time and Cost Efficiency
Developing a crypto exchange platform from scratch requires significant resources, time and technical expertise. So, the Crypto exchange scripts provide a ready-to-use solution, allowing to save time and money by eliminating the need for an extensive development and testing. With this clone script, you can have your exchange up and running it in a fraction of the time.

Customization Options
While the clone scripts provide a pre-built foundation, they also offer the high degree of customization. You can tailor the script to meet your specific business requirements, design preferences and branding. This flexibility allows you to create the unique exchange platform that stands out in the market.

Proven Functionality
Cryptocurrency exchange clone scripts are developed on the basis of successful and established exchange platforms. For instance, the Binance clone script is modeled after the popular Binance exchange. By choosing the clone script, you can leverage the proven functionality and features of an established exchanges, providing a seamless and user-friendly experience to users.

Security and Reliability
Security is of the paramount importance in cryptocurrency industry. Clone scripts are built with robust security measures, ensuring the safety of user funds and data. Additionally, they undergo regular updates and maintenance to address any of the security vulnerabilities. And Choosing a reputable Cryptocurrency Exchange Script will give you peace of mind knowing that your platform is secure and reliable.

Scalability
As the popularity of cryptocurrencies continues to grow, it is essential to have a scalable exchange platform which can handle increasing user demand. Cryptocurrency exchange scripts are designed to be scalable, allowing you to accommodate the growing number of users and transactions without compromising performance.

Now, let us delve into the world of crypto exchange clones and discover the key players that are shaping the future of cryptocurrency trading.

Top 5 Crypto Exchange Clone Scripts

Binance Clone Script
The Binance exchange is one of the most popular and most successful cryptocurrency exchanges globally. The Binance clone script replicates the features and functionalities, which includes buy/sell orders, wallet integration, trading pairs, and advanced security measures. So, by choosing the Binance clone script, you can Create an exchange platform that mirrors the success of Binance.

Coinbase Clone Script
Coinbase is one of the renowned cryptocurrency exchange platforms known for its user-friendly interface and the robust security. The Coinbase clone script provides features such as user management, trading, withdrawals, deposits, and real-time information about buy and sell orders. With this Coinbase clone script, you can develop a exchange platform similar to Coinbase, attracting users with its trusted reputation.

Kucoin Clone Script
Kucoin is a popular crypto exchange known for its wide range of tradable assets and the advanced trading features. The Kucoin clone script provides features such as order wallet integration, management, trading pairs, and an advanced security measures. By choosing this Kucoin clone script, you can launch an crypto excahnge platform.

Kraken Clone Script
Kraken is also one of the popular cryptocurrency exchanges known for their advanced trading features and the robust security. This Kraken clone script replicates its features, allowing you to create a Crypto Exchange platform that caters to traders seeking diverse trading options.

Remitano Clone Script
Remitano is a peer-to-peer crypto exchange known for its escrow system and user-friendly interface. This Remitano clone script replicates its features, allowing you to launch a exchange platform that facilitates secure and seamless peer-to-peer trading. With the Remitano clone script, you can tap into the growing demand for peer-to-peer trading platforms.

Therefore, it is clear that these ready-made solutions are revolutionizing the cryptocurrency trading. And each script offers a unique advantage for businesses entering the market. By harnessing the power of these clone scripts, you can unlock the future of cryptocurrency trading and establish themselves as key players in this dynamic industry.

How to Create Crypto exchange clone script?

Creating a Cryptocurrency Exchange Clone Script involves

  • several steps such as,
  • Research and Planning
  • Design and Development
  • Integration and Testing
  • Deployment and Launch

But, if you feel these development process is quite hard and wanna develop them efficiently in a hassle-free way you can connect with the reliable clone script development company. With their expertise in creating crypto exchange clone scripts, they can assist you in launching a successful exchange platform. Also, they offer customizable and scalable solutions, ensuring that your exchange platform meets your unique business needs. Most importantly, their team of experts will guide you through the entire process, from development to deployment, and provide ongoing support to ensure the smooth operation of your exchange platform.
item.author: lisageorge

item.title: 也许反击 | Maybe Strikes Back
item.description:
在上一章的最后，我们已经说过 Kotlin 的Any? 是一个适当的联合类型。 此外，还有多个方便的运算符和函数可以与Any?一起使用。 作为标准库的一部分存在。 因此，基于这两个陈述，Kotlin 的null处理被推断为“最好的”。 今天我要回顾一下它的弱点。 我们将再次从 Haskell 及其Maybe类型中寻找灵感。 无论如何，很多语言设计者都这样做，为什么我们不能呢？

通用接口

冒着听起来像是破纪录的风险，我想说Maybe的主要力量来自通用Monad类型类。 这导致了统一的使用，从而产生了更具可读性和可重用性的代码。 从 OOP 开发人员的角度来看，这是完全合理的。 我们都听过“代码到接口”的流行口号。 使用类似 Java 的类型重写它可能看起来更清晰： Monad<A> -> Function<A, Monad<B>> -> Monad<B> 。 或者甚至更 Javishly： Monad<B> bind(Monad<A> m, Function<A, Monad<B>> m) 。 这看起来很熟悉吗？ 它应该。 这是 Java 代码库中非常流行的方法，通常称为flatMap 。 但这么流行的方法，问题出在哪里呢？

让我们看一下它的 3 个核心示例：

 Optional<A>.flatMap(Function<A, Optional<B>>) -> Optional<B> Stream<A>.flatMap(Function<A, Stream<B>>) -> Stream<B> CompletableFuture<A>.thenCompose(Function<A, CompletableFuture<B>>) -> CompletableFuture<B>
  • 为了简洁起见，我正在清理一些类型的噪音
  • 还有更多的例子存在，但为了展示这个想法，这些就足够了
  • 类型种类

    我的 OOP 训练要求从相同类型的签名中抽象出一个共同的父对象。 为了实现这一目标，我们需要一个如下所示的接口：

     interface Monad<A> { Monad<B> flatMap(Function<A, Monad<B>> f); }

    这里的问题是它不够通用。 它将返回给我们Monad ，而我们想要的地方是Optional和Stream 。 例如，表达式optional.flatMap(...)将具有父级的Monad<B>类型，而不是子级的Optional<B>类型。 因此，在这样的flatMap之后，我们将无法再链接特定于Optional的方法（显然，没有邪恶的强制转换）。

    如果我们回到 Haskell：

     class Monad m where (>>=) :: ma -> (a -> mb) -> mb

    我们会发现 Haskell 将容器本身声明为泛型类型参数（注意ma中的m ）。 我没能实现它。 如果你成功了，你就可以毁掉这篇文章的推理。 请继续。

    没有必要显示我尝试过的所有不同类型的签名组合。 它们要么不编译，要么（与最初的“天真的” Monad<A>一样）不返回子类型。 最漂亮的例子，只是为了说明我的思维流程：

     interface Monad<A, C extends Monad<A, C>>

    可以通过以下方式扩展：

     sealed interface Maybe<A> extends Monad<A, Maybe<A>> permits Just, Nothing

    这将我们的Monad父类型缩小为正确的Maybe子类型，并使用 Java 17 进行编译。当我们想要从Maybe<A>方法返回Maybe<B>时，就会出现问题。 我们的Monad.flatMap可能如下所示（请注意，我们使用与我们在类级别成功使用的D extends Monad相同的技巧）：

     <B, D extends Monad<B, D>> D flatMap(Function<A, D> f)

    因此， Maybe.flatMap ：

     <B> Maybe<B> flatMap(Function<A, Maybe<B>> f)

    不编译： flatMap(Function<A, Maybe<B>>)' in 'Maybe' clashes with 'flatMap(Function<A, D>)' in 'Monad'; 即使Maybe<B>扩展了Monad<B, Maybe<B> ，编译器也不会用它替换D ，就像之前在类级别上替换C一样。

    一般来说，这是 Java 编译器（以及 Kotlin 编译器）的一个已知限制。 要获得有关此事的更多信息，请搜索更高级的类型。 有多个 Java 库试图添加 HKT，但在撰写本文时，所有这些库都将自己介绍为“尚未准备好生产”。 另外，听起来 HKT 应该成为语言本身的一部分才能足够高效。 因此，不幸的是，目前我们在 Java 世界中陷入了“从代码到实现”的困境。

    命令范式

    在 Haskell 中，通用一元接口允许我们编写声明式代码和（对于纯函数式语言来说令人惊讶的）命令式代码。 让我们看一个简单的例子。 从某个数据库获取某个实体、从某个外部 API 填充一些元数据，然后将结果映射到 DTO 的两种方法。

    声明式：

     fetchDto id = fetchEntityFromDB id >>= populateMeta <&> toDto

    至关重要的：

     fetchDto id = do entity <- fetchEntityFromDB id entityWithMeta <- populateMeta entity let dto = toDto entityWithMeta return dto

    哪种代码看起来更熟悉，因此对于没有功能背景的 Java 开发人员（即 Java 8 之前的普通编码人员）来说更具可读性？ 如果您不熟悉 Haskell，请花点时间理解和比较这两个示例，它们非常简单。

    那么？...你是对的。 第一个！ 至少，这就是我们在 Java 8 中所得到的。我是否听到了对第二种方法的一些微弱的声音？ 别再害怕了，你并不孤单。 有 JVM 上Monad的命令式处理的示例。 不是 Java 本身，而是 Kotlin 的suspend/await让您可以命令式地编写单子代码，就像 Haskell 中do （公平地说，与许多其他async/await实现类似）。 是的，我们正在走出舒适的Maybe外壳，走向更广阔的不同Monad的土地。 但我们会回来的，保证。

    科特林：

     suspend fun fetchDto(id: String): Dto { val entity = fetchEntityFromDb(id) val entityWithMeta = populateMeta(entity) val dto = toDto(entityWithMeta) return dto }

    在 Kotlin 中，我们可以通过使用扩展方法将此函数缩短为一行。 这不是重点。 关键是我们可以在异步逻辑的命令式和声明式范例之间进行选择。

    这很酷，但是假设我们想要添加对其他一些单子类型的支持。 比如我们心爱的T? 我们需要在 Kotlin 中更改多少代码？

     fun fetchDto(id: String): Dto? { val entity = fetchEntityFromDb(id) val entityWithMeta = entity?.let { populateMeta(it) } val dto = entityWithMeta?.let { toDto(it) } return dto }

    呵呵，一点也不干！ 我们需要在 Haskell 中更改多少行？...零，因为我们没有使用任何特定于实现的内容。 向接口编码，还记得那个吗？

    联合之路

    正如我们所知，Scala 中存在另一种数学上正确的可空类型。 它是否遵循单子接口？ 我们可以实施一个吗？

    以下是联合Maybe类型的一种可能实现：

     extension[A] (m: A | Null) def map[B](f: A => B): B | Null = m match case null => null case _ => f(m.nn) def flatMap[B](f: (=> A) => B | Null): B | Null = m match case null => null case _ => f(m.nn) def withFilter(f: A => Boolean): A | Null = m match case null => null case _ => if f(m.nn) then m.nn else null

    接下来是我们很好的示例函数：

     def fetchDto(id: String) = for { entity <- fetchEntityFromDb(id) entityWithMeta <- populateMeta(entity) dto <- toDto(entityWithMeta) } yield dto

    你猜怎么着？ 我们可以将Maybe Monad 更改为实现相同合约的任何其他类型，并且此代码不会更改。

    让我们重新检查上一章中的向后兼容性规则。 在此示例中，我们可以从Entity -> EntityWithMeta | Null缩小populateMeta签名中的返回类型范围。 正如预期的那样，客户端代码没有被破坏。 相同的函数可以正确编译并运行！

    这实际上给我们带来了有趣的副作用。 我们现在可以使用单子管道处理不可空类型：

     val i: Int = 5 val j: Int | Null = i.map(_ - 3)

    我们的A | Null的一个主要缺点我看到的A | Null类型是，如果我们想让它真正有用，我们需要重新发明轮子（即从Option类型重新实现所有功能，如zip和orElse ）。 您还能发现其他问题吗？

    总而言之，到目前为止，我们已经涵盖了从 Java 7 到 Scala 3 联合类型的相当有趣的演变路径。 从理论的角度来看，后者现在似乎优于可用的替代方案（这并不意味着它是理想的，不是吗？）。 在下一章中（如果它出版的话），我计划进一步抽象出“​​缺席价值”的概念。 Null is too specific. Null太具体了。 "Code to interface", still remember that one? “代码到界面”，还记得那一篇吗？



原文:

In the end of the previous chapter, we've stated that Kotlin's Any? is a proper union type. Also, multiple handy operators and functions to work with Any? exist as part of the standard library. So, based on these two statements, Kotlin's null handling has been inferred to be "the best". Today I'm going to review its weaker points. Again, we'll be looking at Haskell and its Maybe type for inspiration. A lot of language designers do this anyway, why can't we?

Universal Interface

With a risk of sounding like a broken record, I would state that the main power of Maybe comes from the universal Monad type class. Maybe itself may not be the best answer to nullability question, but its interface answers to dozens of other questions at the same time. This leads to a unified usage and thus to more readable and re-usable code. And it's completely reasonable from the OOP developer point of view. We all have heard the popular mantra of "code to interface". And here Haskell does exactly that.

The key function of monad, bind, has signature m a -> (a -> m b) -> m b. Rewriting this using Java-like types may look clearer: Monad<A> -> Function<A, Monad<B>> -> Monad<B>. Or even more Javishly: Monad<B> bind(Monad<A> m, Function<A, Monad<B>> m). Does this look familiar? It should. It's a quite popular method among Java codebases, commonly known as flatMap. But where is the problem with such a popular method?

Let's see 3 core examples of it:

Optional<A>.flatMap(Function<A, Optional<B>>) -> Optional<B> Stream<A>.flatMap(Function<A, Stream<B>>) -> Stream<B> CompletableFuture<A>.thenCompose(Function<A, CompletableFuture<B>>) -> CompletableFuture<B>
  • I'm cleaning up some type noise for brevity here
  • A lot more examples exist in the wild, but to showcase the idea these are enough
  • CompletableFuture is my favorite: to make matters worse, it doesn't even try to conform to usual naming

Kinds of Types

My OOP training screams to abstract a common parent out of the same type signatures. To achieve this, we need an interface looking like the following one:

interface Monad<A> { Monad<B> flatMap(Function<A, Monad<B>> f);
}

The problem here is that it's not generic enough. It will return us Monads where we would want Optionals and Streams. For example, expression optional.flatMap(...) will have parent's type of Monad<B> not child's Optional<B>. So, after such flatMap we wouldn't be able to chain Optional-specific methods anymore (without unholy casts, obviously).

If we go back to Haskell:

class Monad m where (>>=) :: m a -> (a -> m b) -> m b

We'll spot that Haskell declares container itself as a generic type parameter (note the m in m a). Can Java do the same? I wasn't able to achieve it. If you succeed, you can destroy the reasoning of this article. Please go ahead.

No point in showing all the different type signature combinations I've tried. They either do not compile, or (as with initial "naive" Monad<A>) do not return child type. The prettiest example, just to illustrate my thought-flow:

interface Monad<A, C extends Monad<A, C>>

Can be extended by:

sealed interface Maybe<A> extends Monad<A, Maybe<A>> permits Just, Nothing

Which narrows our Monad parent type to proper Maybe child type and compiles using Java 17. The problem arises when we want to return Maybe<B> from Maybe<A> method. Our Monad.flatMap could look like the following (note that we're using the same trick of D extends Monad we used successfully on class level):

<B, D extends Monad<B, D>> D flatMap(Function<A, D> f)

And, consequently, Maybe.flatMap:

<B> Maybe<B> flatMap(Function<A, Maybe<B>> f)

Which doesn't compile with: flatMap(Function<A, Maybe<B>>)' in 'Maybe' clashes with 'flatMap(Function<A, D>)' in 'Monad';. Even though Maybe<B> extends Monad<B, Maybe<B>, the compiler doesn't substitute D with it, as it did with C on class level previously.

Generally speaking, it's a known limitation of the Java compiler (as well as Kotlin's). To get more information on the matter, search for higher-kinded types. There are multiple Java libraries trying to add HKT, but at the moment of writing all of them introduce themselves as "not production-ready" ones. Also, it sounds like HKT should be part of a language itself to be efficient enough. So, unfortunately for now we are stuck with "code to implementation" in Java-world.

Imperative Paradigm

In Haskell, the universal monadic interface allows us to write both declarative and (surprisingly for a purely functional language) imperative code. Let's look at a simple example. Two ways of fetching some entity from some database, populating some metadata from some external API, and then mapping the result to a DTO.

Declarative:

fetchDto id = fetchEntityFromDB id >>= populateMeta <&> toDto

Imperative:

fetchDto id = do entity <- fetchEntityFromDB id entityWithMeta <- populateMeta entity let dto = toDto entityWithMeta return dto

Which code looks more familiar and thus more readable for Java developers without no functional background (i.e. for an average coder prior to Java 8)? If you are not familiar with Haskell, take your time to comprehend and compare both examples, they are really simple.

So?.. Right you are. The first one! At least, that is what we've got in Java 8. Am I hearing some weak voices for the second approach? Fear no more, you're not alone in this. There are examples of imperative handling of Monads on the JVM. Not in Java itself, but Kotlin's suspend/await lets you write monadic code imperatively, exactly like do in Haskell (to be fair, similarly to lots of other async/await implementations). Yes, we are stepping out of our cozy Maybe-only shell to a wider land of different Monads here. But we'll return, promise.

Java 8:

CompletableFuture<Dto> fetchDto(String id) { return fetchEntityFromDb(id) .thenCompose(this::populateMeta) .thenApply(this::toDto);
}

Kotlin:

suspend fun fetchDto(id: String): Dto { val entity = fetchEntityFromDb(id) val entityWithMeta = populateMeta(entity) val dto = toDto(entityWithMeta) return dto
}

In Kotlin, we can shorten this function to a single line by using extension methods. That's not the point. The point is that we have a choice between imperative and declarative paradigms for asynchronous logic.

That's cool, but let's say that we want to add support for some other monadic type. For example, our beloved T?. How much code would we need to change in Kotlin?

fun fetchDto(id: String): Dto? { val entity = fetchEntityFromDb(id) val entityWithMeta = entity?.let { populateMeta(it) } val dto = entityWithMeta?.let { toDto(it) } return dto
}

Huh, not DRY at all! How many lines we would need to change in Haskell?.. Zero, since we aren't using anything implementation-specific. Coding to an interface, remember that one?

The Union Way

As we already know, another mathematically correct nullable type resides in Scala. Does it adhere to the monadic interface? Can we implement one?

Scala lacks an explicit trait for Monad, however has an implicit interface that is used by for-comprehensions, which in turn are extremely similar to Haskell's do blocks we've already seen. Here is one possible implementation for unionized Maybe type:

extension[A] (m: A | Null) def map[B](f: A => B): B | Null = m match case null => null case _ => f(m.nn) def flatMap[B](f: (=> A) => B | Null): B | Null = m match case null => null case _ => f(m.nn) def withFilter(f: A => Boolean): A | Null = m match case null => null case _ => if f(m.nn) then m.nn else null

Next is our good ol' sample function:

def fetchDto(id: String) = for { entity <- fetchEntityFromDb(id) entityWithMeta <- populateMeta(entity) dto <- toDto(entityWithMeta)
} yield dto

And guess what? We can change our Maybe Monad to any other type implementing the same contract and this code will not change.

Let's re-check backward compatibility rules from the previous chapter. In this example, we can narrow down the return type in the signature of the populateMeta from Entity -> EntityWithMeta | Null to Entity -> EntityWithMeta. And as expected, client code is not broken. The same function compiles and works correctly!

This actually leads us to the interesting side effect. We now can work with non-nullable types using monadic pipelines:

val i: Int = 5
val j: Int | Null = i.map(_ - 3)

The one major drawback of our A | Null type I see is that if we want to make it actually useful, we need to re-invent the wheel (i.e. re-implement all the functionality, like zip and orElse, from the Option type). Can you spot any other problems?

All in all, by this moment we've covered quite an interesting evolutionary path from Java 7 to Scala 3 union types. From a theoretical standpoint, the latter now seem superior to the available alternatives (which doesn't mean that it's ideal, does it?). In the next chapter (if it ever gets published), I'm planning to abstract out the notion of an "absent value" even more. Null is too specific. "Code to interface", still remember that one?
item.author: vchernetskyi993

item.title: 使用 AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) 通过 HTTPS 保护 Lambda 与 ELB 连接的安全 | Securing Lambda to ELB Connections with HTTPS using AWS Certificate Manager (ACM)
item.description:
铃木敦志

发布于 7 月 1 日

我在东京的一家初创公司担任人工智能工程师。 由于我们只有两名工程师（包括我自己），我发现自己不仅负责前端（React）和后端（NestJS）开发，还负责基础设施（AWS）开发。

在对应用进行架构改造之前进行研究时，我们发现Lambda到另一个VPC的ELB的连接方式是HTTP，存在安全风险，我们决定立即进行改造。

由于我们之前没有使用 HTTPS 的经验，因此我们总结了我们的调查和实施，以供提醒。 如果内容有不足的地方，请大家在评论区指出，不胜感激。

在深入实现之前，我们先来清楚地了解 Lambda 函数使用 HTTPS 连接 ELB 时的流程：

  1. 启动连接：Lambda 函数启动与 ELB 的 HTTPS 终端节点的连接。
  2. 证书呈现：ELB 向 Lambda 函数呈现其证书（以及公钥）。 该证书由 ACM 或其他证书管理器颁发，并在 ELB 上配置。
  3. 证书验证：Lambda 函数（更准确地说，是 Lambda 函数内代码中的 HTTP 客户端库，例如 Axios）验证证书。 它确认证书是由受信任的证书颁发机构 (CA) 颁发的、它仍然有效，并且它确实属于尝试连接的服务器 (ELB)。
  4. 创建和共享加密密钥：验证证书后，Lambda 函数将生成会话密钥（共享密钥）。 该会话密钥使用 ELB 的公钥加密并发送给 ELB。 然后，ELB 使用自己的私钥解密加密的会话密钥。
  5. 安全通信的启动：ELB 和 Lambda 函数之间的所有通信均使用共享会话密钥进行加密。 这确保即使第三方拦截此通信，如果没有私钥，他们也无法读取信息。

执行

了解了HTTPS连接的流程后，实现需要以下步骤：

  1. 颁发SSL/TLS证书
  2. 在 ELB 上创建新的监听器

由于基本任务是将 HTTP 连接升级到 HTTPS，因此需要在 Route53、AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) 和 ELB 上进行配置。

使用 AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) 颁发将附加到 ELB 的 SSL/TLS 证书。

为 ELB 创建自定义域

在ACM中颁发证书时，需要关联ELB域。 但是，您无法为 AWS 已拥有的域颁发证书，例如.elb.amazonaws.com 。 因此，您需要创建一个新的自定义域（例如mywebsite.com ）。

由于我们有一个现有的域（例如mywebsite.com ），因此我们在 Route53 托管区域中创建一条新记录（例如service.mywebsite.com ）并将其映射到 ELB URL。

  1. 在 Route 53 仪表板中，单击“DNS 管理”。
  2. 单击“托管区域”。
  3. 单击相关托管区域的行（例如mywebsite.com ）。
  4. 在右侧窗格中，单击“创建记录”。
  5. 在新记录配置屏幕中，执行以下操作：

在“记录名称”中，输入您要配置的子域（例如service ）。 对于“记录类型”，选择 A - IPv4 地址。 对于“值/路由流量至”，选择弹性负载均衡器的别名。

在“选择区域”中，选择ELB所在区域（例如ap-northeast-1 ）。


在“选择 ELB”中，选择目标 ELB（例如alb-12345678 ）。
点击“创建记录”。

申请证书

使用创建的自定义域，您现在可以从 ACM 请求证书。 设置如下并点击“请求”。

如果您在上一步中选择了 DNS 验证，则需要将 ACM 提供的 CNAME 记录添加到 Route53 以验证域所有权。

申请证书后，打开详情页面，复制CNAME名称和CNAME值。

这样就完成了 Route53 的设置，以使用 ACM 提供的 CNAME 记录来验证域所有权。 一段时间后，返回ACM管理控制台，确认证书状态已变为【已颁发】。

从ELB的监听器设置中将颁发的证书注册到ELB。

导航到 ELB 设置屏幕，选择目标负载均衡器，然后移至“侦听器”选项卡。 在这里，单击“编辑”并选择“添加侦听器”以添加 HTTPS 侦听器。

对于目标组，请选择一个现有组。 显示HTTP表示ELB到后端服务的连接是HTTP。 虽然这也应该转换为 HTTPS，但为了解释起见，它保持原样（ELB 将是 SSL 终止点）。

此外，在安全策略中，您还可以定义 Lambda 和 ELB 之间通信所使用的 SSL/TLS 协议和密码。 这次，我们选择了标记为(recommended)的。

在“默认SSL/TLS证书”部分选择本次颁发的证书。

创建侦听器后，与 ELB 连接的 HTTPS 转换就完成了。

一旦被暂停，suzuki0430 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，suzuki0430将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，suzuki0430 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己才能访问。

如果 suzuki0430 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 suzuki0430：

使 suzuki0430 的所有帖子不那么明显

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 suzuki0430 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Atsushi Suzuki

Posted on Jul 1

 

I'm working as an AI engineer at a startup in Tokyo. As we only have two engineers including myself, I find myself handling not just frontend (React) and backend (NestJS) development, but also infrastructure (AWS) development.

While conducting research prior to an architectural retrofit of the application, we discovered a security risk in that the connection from Lambda to the ELB of another VPC was HTTP, and we decided to make the retrofit immediately.

Since we had no experience working with HTTPS before, we have summarized our investigation and implementation as a reminder. If there are any inadequacies in the contents, I would appreciate it if you could point them out in the comments section.

The Flow of HTTPS Connection Between Lambda and ELB

Before diving into the implementation, let's get a clear understanding of the flow when a Lambda function connects to an ELB using HTTPS:

  1. Initiating the Connection: The Lambda function initiates a connection to the ELB's HTTPS endpoint.
  2. Certificate Presentation: The ELB presents its certificate (along with the public key) to the Lambda function. This certificate is issued by ACM or another certificate manager and is configured on the ELB.
  3. Certificate Verification: The Lambda function (more accurately, the HTTP client library such as Axios in the code within the Lambda function) verifies the certificate. It confirms that the certificate is issued by a trusted Certificate Authority (CA), that it is still valid, and that it indeed belongs to the server (ELB) to which the connection is being attempted.
  4. Creation and Sharing of Encryption Key: Once the certificate is verified, the Lambda function generates a session key (shared secret key). This session key is encrypted with the ELB's public key and sent to the ELB. The ELB then decrypts the encrypted session key using its own private key.
  5. Initiation of Secure Communication: All communication between the ELB and the Lambda function is encrypted using the shared session key. This ensures that even if a third party intercepts this communication, they would be unable to read the information without the private key.

Implementation

Having understood the flow of HTTPS connections, the following steps are required for the implementation:

  1. Issuance of SSL/TLS Certificate
  2. Creating a new listener on ELB

Since the basic task is to upgrade an HTTP connection to HTTPS, configurations on Route53, AWS Certificate Manager (ACM), and ELB will be required.

1. Issuance of SSL/TLS Certificate

Issue the SSL/TLS certificate that will be attached to the ELB using AWS Certificate Manager (ACM).

Creation of a Custom Domain for ELB

When issuing a certificate in ACM, you need to associate the ELB domain. However, you can't issue a certificate for a domain that AWS already owns, such as .elb.amazonaws.com. Therefore, you will need to create a new custom domain (e.g., mywebsite.com).

Since we have an existing domain (e.g., mywebsite.com), we create a new record (e.g., service.mywebsite.com) in the Route53 hosted zone and map it to the ELB URL.

  1. In the Route 53 dashboard, click on "DNS management".
  2. Click on "Hosted zones".
  3. Click on the row of the relevant hosted zone (e.g., mywebsite.com).
  4. In the right-side pane, click on "Create Record".
  5. In the new record configuration screen, do the following:

a. In "Record name", enter the subdomain you want to configure (e.g., service). b. For "Record type", select A - IPv4 address. c. For "Value/Route traffic to", select Alias to Elastic Load Balancer.

d. In "Choose Region", select the region where the ELB is located (e.g., ap-northeast-1).


e. In "Choose ELB", select the target ELB (e.g., alb-12345678).
f. Click on "Create records".

Requesting the Certificate

Using the custom domain created, you can now request a certificate from ACM. Set as follows and click "Request".

DNS Validation

If you chose DNS validation in the previous step, you'll need to add the CNAME record provided by ACM to Route53 to verify domain ownership.

After requesting the certificate, open the details page and copy the CNAME name and CNAME value.

This completes the setup for Route53 to use the CNAME record provided by ACM to verify domain ownership. After some time, return to the ACM management console and confirm that the status of the certificate has changed to [Issued].

2. Creating a new listener on ELB

Register the issued certificate to ELB from the listener settings of ELB.

Navigate to the ELB settings screen, select the target Load Balancer, and then move to the "Listeners" tab. Here, click on "Edit" and select "Add listener" to add an HTTPS listener.

For the Target Group, select an existing one. The display of HTTP means that the connection from ELB to the backend service is HTTP. Although this should also be converted to HTTPS, it remains as it is for the sake of explanation (ELB will be the SSL termination point).

Also, in the Security Policy, you define the SSL/TLS protocol and cipher used in communication between Lambda and ELB. This time, we have chosen the one that is marked as (recommended).

In the part of Default SSL/TLS certificate, select the certificate issued this time.

Once the listener is created, the conversion to HTTPS for connections to ELB is complete.
